                  "content": "        \"contents\": \"---\\ntitle: \\\"Text-to-Speech Evaluation\\\"\\ndescription: \\\"Compare and evaluate TTS providers for voice quality and latency\\\"\\n---\\n\\n# Text-to-Speech Evaluation\\n\\nPense allows you to evaluate multiple Text-to-Speech (TTS) providers simultaneously using your own text samples. This helps you find the best provider for voice quality and latency.\\n\\n## What You'll Learn\\n\\n- Add text samples for synthesis\\n- Compare multiple TTS providers side-by-side\\n- Listen to generated audio\\n- Analyze quality metrics (LLM Judge) and latency (TTFB, Processing Time)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 1: Navigate to TTS Evaluations\\n\\nFrom the sidebar, click on **Text-to-Speech** to view all your TTS evaluations. Click **New TTS evaluation** to create a new one.\\n\\n&amp;lt;Frame&amp;gt;\\n  &amp;lt;img src=\\\"/images/tts_overview.png\\\" alt=\\\"TTS Evaluations List\\\" /&amp;gt;\\n&amp;lt;/Frame&amp;gt;\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 2: Configure Your Evaluation\\n\\n### Settings Tab\\n\\n**Select Language:**\\n- English\\n- Hindi\\n\\n**Select Provi [... omitted end of long line]"

                  "lineNumber": 5255,
                  "content": "        \"contents\": \"export default function TTSPage() {\\n  const router = useRouter();\\n  const { data: session } = useSession();\\n  const backendAccessToken = (session as any)?.backendAccessToken;\\n  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);\\n  const [jobs, setJobs] = useState&amp;lt;TTSJob[]&amp;gt;([]);\\n  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);\\n  const [error, setError] = useState&amp;lt;string | null&amp;gt;(null);\\n  const [sortOrder, setSortOrder] = useState&amp;lt;\\\"asc\\\" | \\\"desc\\\"&amp;gt;(\\\"desc\\\");\\n\\n  // Set page title\\n  useEffect(() =&amp;gt; {\\n    document.title = \\\"Text to Speech | Pense\\\";\\n  }, []);\\n\\n  // Fetch TTS jobs\\n  useEffect(() =&amp;gt; {\\n    const fetchJobs = async () =&amp;gt; {\\n      if (!backendAccessToken) return;\\n\\n      try {\\n        setIsLoading(true);\\n        setError(null);\\n        const backendUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL;\\n        if (!backendUrl) {\\n          throw new Error(\\\"BACKEND_URL environment variable is not set\\\");\\n        }\\ [... omitted end of long line]"
                },

                  "lineNumber": 5307,

                  "content": "        \"contents\": \"---\\ntitle: \\\"Speech-to-Text Evaluation\\\"\\ndescription: \\\"Compare and evaluate STT providers for accuracy and latency\\\"\\n---\\n\\n# Speech-to-Text Evaluation\\n\\nPense allows you to evaluate multiple Speech-to-Text (STT) providers simultaneously using your own audio samples. This helps you find the best provider for your specific language and use case.\\n\\n## What You'll Learn\\n\\n- Upload audio samples with reference transcriptions\\n- Compare multiple STT providers side-by-side\\n- Analyze accuracy metrics (WER, String Similarity, LLM Judge)\\n- Measure latency (TTFB, Processing Time)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Step 1: Navigate to STT Evaluations\\n\\nFrom the sidebar, click on **Speech-to-Text** to view all your STT evaluations.                   "lineNumber": 3299,
                  "content": "            \"text\": \"Pense allows you to evaluate multiple Text-to-Speech (TTS) providers simultaneously using your own text samples. This helps you find the best provider for voice quality and latency.\","
                },

                  "lineNumber": 5255,
                  "content": "        \"contents\": \"export default function TTSPage() {\\n  const router = useRouter();\\n  const { data: session } = useSession();\\n  const backendAccessToken = (session as any)?.backendAccessToken;\\n  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);\\n  const [jobs, setJobs] = useState&amp;lt;TTSJob[]&amp;gt;([]);\\n  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);\\n  const [error, setError] = useState&amp;lt;string | null&amp;gt;(null);\\n  const [sortOrder, setSortOrder] = useState&amp;lt;\\\"asc\\\" | \\\"desc\\\"&amp;gt;(\\\"desc\\\");\\n\\n  // Set page title\\n  useEffect(() =&amp;gt; {\\n    document.title = \\\"Text to Speech | Pense\\\";\\n  }, []);\\n\\n  // Fetch TTS jobs\\n  useEffect(() =&amp;gt; {\\n    const fetchJobs = async () =&amp;gt; {\\n      if (!backendAccessToken) return;\\n\\n      try {\\n        setIsLoading(true);\\n        setError(null);\\n        const backendUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL;\\n        if (!backendUrl) {\\n          throw new Error(\\\"BACKEND_URL environment variable is not set\\\");\\n        }\\ [... omitted end of long line]"

                {
                  "lineNumber": 8367,
                  "content": "Found it! The layout.tsx has a &#96;metadata&#96; export with &#96;title: \"Pense - Voice Agent Evaluation\"&#96;. This is the default title set at the layout level. When the page is first loaded, it first shows this metadata title, and then the &#96;useEffect&#96; runs to update it.The issue is that when switching tabs, the &#96;headerState&#96; might be getting reset or the useEffect is not triggering properly. Let me look at the agent detail page's useEffect more closely.Looking at the useEffect:&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8371,
                  "content": "    document.title = &#96;${headerState.agentName} - ${tabName} | Pense&#96;;"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8373,
                  "content": "    document.title = \"Agent | Pense\";"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8376,
                  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;The dependencies include &#96;headerState?.agentName&#96; and &#96;headerState?.activeTab&#96;, so when &#96;activeTab&#96; changes, the effect should run. But wait - the title format \"Pense - Voice agent evaluation\" (with \"Pense\" first) is different from what we set (\"Agent | Pense\" with \"Pense\" last). This is the metadata title from layout.tsx.The problem might be that:1. When the tab changes, something resets headerState to null temporarily"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8377,
                  "content": "2. The useEffect then sets \"Agent | Pense\" because headerState is null"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8378,
                  "content": "3. But actually, the user said they see \"Pense - Voice agent evaluation\" which is the layout metadata"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8380,
                  "content": "Actually wait - the user said \"Pense - Voice agent evaluation\" but that's the metadata format. The metadata title is \"Pense - Voice Agent Evaluation\" (capitalized differently). Let me check if there's something resetting the title."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8386,
                  "content": "The most straightforward solution is to update the layout metadata to use a simpler default like \"Pense\" instead of the current fallback, then let individual pages define their own specific titles. This way, the base layout provides a sensible default without interfering with page-level metadata.&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8390,
                  "content": "Found the issue! The &#96;layout.tsx&#96; has a default metadata title &#96;\"Pense - Voice Agent Evaluation\"&#96;. When you switch tabs, Next.js's app router might be briefly restoring this default title before the &#96;useEffect&#96; runs."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8407,
                  "content": "-   title: \"Pense - Voice Agent Evaluation\","
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8409,
                  "content": "+     default: \"Pense\","
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8423,
                  "content": "Now the default title is just \"Pense\" which is much better as a fallback. The template &#96;\"%s\"&#96; means pages can override the entire title.But the real issue might still persist because the title is being set via &#96;document.title&#96; in useEffect, which may have a race condition with tab switching. Let me also check if there's an issue with how the headerState is being updated when tabs change."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8474,
                  "content": "Let me think about this differently. The user says when they switch tabs, the title becomes \"Pense - Voice agent evaluation\". This is the old metadata title. After my change, the default would be just \"Pense\". But the user's current version still has the old metadata."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 8601,

                  "lineNumber": 340,

                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 344,
                  "content": "+ Pense is a comprehensive platform for creating, configuring, testing, and evaluating voice agents. It provides tools for unit testing individual components (STT, TTS) and end-to-end simulation testing with configurable personas and scenarios."

                  "lineNumber": 683,

                {
                  "lineNumber": 795,

                },

                  "lineNumber": 799,
                  "content": "+ ## What is Pense?"
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 802,
                  "content": "+ Pense is a comprehensive platform for building, configuring, testing, and evaluating **voice-based AI agents**. It enables teams to create conversational agents that can handle voice interactions (phone calls, voice assistants) and rigorously test them before deployment."
                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 860,
                  "content": "+ Organizations building voice agents (customer support bots, IVR systems, voice assistants) use Pense to:"

                },
                {
                  "lineNumber": 1312,
                  "content": "+ Instead of manual testing, Pense uses AI-powered simulation:"
